properties.remark: SEVERAL SMALL SHEDS WERE DESTROYED ALONG WITH SOME LARGE TREES BLOWN OVER NEAR HIGHWAY 103.

A HOUSE SUSTAINED DAMAGE. SEVERAL ROOF SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF. THE TOP OF THE CHIMNEY WAS BLOWN OFF. A DOOR WAS BLOWN IN.

ROOF BLOWN OFF NEEDS CREEK BAPTIST CHURCH EAST OF SPRINGHILL. TREES AND POWERLINES REPORTED DOWN IN THE AREA AS WELL.
THERE WAS A 2 MILE SWATH OF DAMAGE THAT STARTED AT ARKLA VALLEY AND HIGHWAY 287. MULTIPLE STRUCTURES WERE DESTROYED, ROOFS WERE TORE OFF, AND TREES AND POWER LINES WERE DOWN.

THE ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF NEEDS CREEK BAPTIST CHURCH EAST OF SPRINGHILL. THIS APPEARS TO BE THE AREA WITH THE WORST DAMAGE. TREES AND POWERLINES WERE REPORTED DOWN IN THE AREA AS WELL. DAMAGE WAS CAUSED BY AN EF1 TORNADO, WITH WINDS AROUND 100 MPH. MORE DETAILS WILL FOLLOW LATER.
THE AWNING OF A GAS STATION AT HIGHWAY 65 AND 425 WAS DESTROYED. POWER OUTAGES WERE REPORTED ON THE EAST SIDE OF TOWN NEAR THE AIRPORT.
SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED. SOME TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN. THE DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS OF 60-80 MPH.
CHICKEN HOUSE ROOFS WERE DAMAGED AND REMOVED. OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED. THE DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS IN EXCESS OF 70 MPH.

NWS DAMAGE SURVEY CONCLUDES TREE DAMAGE NEAR SUMPTER AR WAS THE RESULT OF DAMAGING THUNDERSTORM WINDS. DAMAGE WAS CONCENTRATED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF AR-8... BRADLEY 42 RD... AND JIMMY SENN RD. SEVERAL TREES WERE UPROOTED... MOSTLY EAST OF AR-8... WITH A COUPLE OF TREES SNAPPED. A METAL OUTBUILDING WAS DAMAGED AT A HOME ACROSS FROM SUMPTER METHODIST CHURCH. SOME FENCE DAMAGE WAS NOTED AS WELL... BUT NO OTHER SIGNIFICANT STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED. ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WIND SPEED IS 90 MPH.

**THIS IS A DELAYED REPORT** A WEAK EF-0 TORNADO WAS OBSERVED YESTERDAY IN FAR NORTHEAST PRAIRIE COUNTY. THE TORNADO WAS A LANDSPOUT TORNADO AND LASTED FOR APPROXIMATELY 14 MINUTES AND MOVED VERY SLOWLY TO THE SOUTHWEST AT ABOUT 4 MPH ALONG A 1 MILE PATH. THE ONLY DAMAGE OBSERVED WAS TO A SMALL FIELD PUMPING STATION.

DAMAGE WAS REPORTED ON LAKE GREESON AND IN AREAS TO THE SOUTH NEAR MURFREESBORO. ON LAKE GREESON DAMAGE WAS REPORTED TO THE ROOF ON THE MARINA OF DOCKS E AND D. DAMAGE NEAR MURFREESBORO WAS SEVERAL TREES BLOWN DOWN. A REVIEW OF VIDEOS TAKEN AT THE TIME REVEAL A CIRCULATION OVER LAKE GREESON WHEN THE DAMAGE OCCURRED. FROM THE VIDEO EVIDENCE AND A CAREFUL REVIEW OF RADAR DATA, A STRONG MICROBURST OCCURRED IN BETWEEN DAISY AND MURFREESBORO RESULTING IN A SUSTAINED CIRCULATION OVER LAKE GREESON THAT

DAMAGE WAS REPORTED ON LAKE GREESON AND IN AREAS TO THE SOUTH NEAR MURFREESBORO. ON LAKE GREESON DAMAGE WAS REPORTED TO THE ROOF ON THE MARINA OF DOCKS E AND D. DAMAGE NEAR MURFREESBORO WAS SEVERAL TREES BLOWN DOWN. A REVIEW OF VIDEOS TAKEN AT THE TIME REVEAL A CIRCULATION OVER LAKE GREESON WHEN THE DAMAGE OCCURRED. FROM THE VIDEO EVIDENCE AND A CAREFUL REVIEW OF RADAR DATA, A STRONG MICROBURST OCCURRED IN BETWEEN DAISY AND MURFREESBORO RESULTING IN A SUSTAINED CIRCULATION OVER LAKE GREESON THAT, WHEN OVER LAND, IS TYPICALLY REFERRED TO AS A GUSTNADO. THIS APPEARED TO BE A GUSTNADO OVER WATER. WITH THE DAMAGE BEING INITIATED BY DOWNDRAFT, IT IS CONSIDERED THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE.

TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN IN THE CITY OF HOUSTON, AND COUNTYWIDE AS WELL. IN THE CITY OF HOUSTON, A WELL BUILT TRACTOR SHED WAS DESTROYED AND A GARAGE HAD ITS TOP BLOWN OFF BY POWERFUL THUNDERSTORM WINDS OF 80 TO 90 MPH. WIND DAMAGE OF 60 TO 70 MPH DAMAGED TREES AND POWERLINES ACROSS THE COUNTY, AND HOUSTON APPEARED TO BE THE AREA HARDEST HIT. A HORSE WAS KILLED BY A LIGHTNING STRIKE IN THE CITY OF HOUSTON AS WELL.

SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST SOUTHWEST OF CALICO MOUNTAIN AFTER CROSSING REVEILLE VALLEY ROAD. THIS IS ABOUT FOUR MILES SOUTH OF PARIS.
